title: pedicularis sceptrum-carolinum subsp. pubescens
intro: Pedicularis sceptrum-carolinum subsp. pubescens, also known as Lousewort, is a plant species belonging to the balsamroot or lousewort family, native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Lousewort include reducing inflammation and relieving congestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory conditions. Lousewort can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for various methods of consumption to harness its medicinal properties.
